page: Amazon Web Services m8id.large is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is provided via a 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance demonstrates strong single-threaded capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2162.0 and high single-threaded compression throughput, including 33.96 GB/sec in gzip compression and 14.43 GB/sec in bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are generally weak. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10% with a score of 2158.88, and memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks register in the bottom 25%. Similarly, database operations, static web serving, and LLM inference tasks show lower-tier performance.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8id.large offers a modern DDR5 memory architecture and dedicated CPU allocation alongside local NVMe storage, which reduces reliance on network-attached storage for temporary files. However, the limitation of a single physical core restricts its utility for highly parallelized workloads. The instance is suited for single-threaded applications, basic text processing, lightweight utility scripting, and development tasks that benefit from fast local scratch space but do not require high multi-core throughput or massive memory bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.large is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 2 vCPUs on 1 physical core, running at 3.9 GHz. It includes 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz and a 118 GB local NVMe SSD instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers strong single-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2162.0 and high single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are limited, with stress-ng multi-core metrics falling into the bottom 10%. The instance offers a dedicated CPU allocation and fast local storage, making it qualitatively cost-effective for workloads that do not require extensive parallel processing. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, and lightweight development tasks requiring local scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.large is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance alongside limited multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.large is a general-purpose Nitro instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 118 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: A general-purpose instance pairing strong single-threaded Intel CPU performance with fast local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 2 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
8.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
118 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression benchmarks
Limited multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities
Suited for single-threaded applications and lightweight development tasks
